name: ASAP — As-Static-As-Possible
summary: Raphaël Proust's ASAP (Cambridge PhD 2017) is fully automatic, with no annotations and no GC: dataflow analyses (usage + aliasing) emit per-program-point deallocation instructions at compile time, falling back to bounded runtime scans only when static info is insufficient. Subsumes both linear types and Tofte-Talpin regions. The most aggressive no-annotation, no-GC point in the design space.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Raphaël Proust's **ASAP** (Cambridge PhD 2017) is fully automatic, with no annotations and no GC: dataflow analyses (usage + aliasing) emit per-program-point deallocation instructions at compile time, falling back to bounded runtime scans only when static info is insufficient.

It is the **most aggressive no-annotation, no-GC point in the design space**: where Tofte-Talpin/MLKit infer region structure but assume a stack discipline, ASAP infers a per-program-point deallocation schedule directly, with no syntactic concept of "region" exposed to the source language.

sections.title: Subsumes linear types and Tofte-Talpin regions
sections.content:
ASAP **subsumes both linear types and Tofte-Talpin regions**: given a linear/region-correct program, ASAP emits equivalent free instructions. Where static analysis can't decide, it inserts specialised scan-and-deallocate code (bounded, unlike a tracing GC). The bounded runtime fallback is the key design move: instead of a global tracing collector, ASAP emits *targeted* per-program-point scans whose work is statically bounded by the analysis.

This lets ASAP recover Tofte-Talpin behaviour when the program's lifetime structure is region-shaped, recover linear-type behaviour when the program is linear, and degrade gracefully (rather than catastrophically) when the program needs neither.

sections.title: LLVM backend and Boehm-GC comparison
sections.content:
Followed up by **Nathan Corbyn's Cambridge Part II (2020)** with an LLVM backend and benchmarks vs. Boehm GC. The evaluation provides empirical evidence that the ASAP point in the design space is reachable: a real implementation, with a real backend, and competitive numbers against a well-tuned conservative tracing collector.

Status (as of 2026-04): research-grade. ASAP has not been adopted as a production compilation strategy by any mainstream language, but it is regularly cited as a feasibility proof for fully automatic, GC-free memory management.
